Wyoming Dietitian License Reciprocity & How to Apply

Already licensed elsewhere? Start with compact status, endorsement requirements, fees, and your likely transfer timeline.

Last verified 2026-02-25
Compact Enacted - Not Yet Active

Reciprocity in Wyoming for Dietitians

Wyoming has enacted the Dietitian Compact, but compact privileges are not yet issuing. Until implementation goes live, apply by endorsement through the state board.

Once active, compact participation should reduce or eliminate the need for a separate transfer application.

Endorsement fee
$200
Processing time
1 to 2 Weeks
Fingerprinting
Required
Temporary license
Available
See endorsement steps Official board portal

Endorsement Application Steps

  1. Complete Education & Supervised Practice

    Earn a master's degree and complete coursework from an ACEND-accredited program. Finish a minimum of 1,000 hours of supervised dietetics practice.

  2. Pass the CDR Examination

    Successfully pass the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR) Registration Examination for Dietitians. Verification of current RD status satisfies this.

  3. Submit the Application to the Board

    Complete the application form, gather required proof of citizenship, and submit the packet along with the $200 fee to the Wyoming Dietetics Licensing Board.

  4. Provide Verification Documents

    Ensure that the Commission on Dietetic Registration sends official verification of your active RD status directly to the Board.

Quick Facts

Total fee
$200
Processing time
1 to 2 Weeks
Renewal fee
$200
Renewal cycle
Every 2 years + CE hours

Required Documents

  • Official Application Packet: Application accurately filled out along with proof of U.S. citizenship or lawful presence.
  • CDR Registration Verification: Proof of active registration sent directly from the CDR.

Board Contact

Board
Wyoming Dietetics Licensing Board
Department
Wyoming Department of Administration & Information
Phone
(307) 777-7788
Email
wyeboard@wyo.gov

Visit official board portal

Renewal

Renewal fee is $200. Renewal cycle: Every 2 years + CE hours.

Wyoming Dietitian Reciprocity FAQ

How much does a Wyoming dietitian license cost?

The initial license application fee is $200.

When does the Wyoming license need to be renewed?

Wyoming dietitian licenses expire biennially. The renewal fee is $200.

Is licensure required to practice in Wyoming?

Yes, Wyoming has a Practice Act that legally mandates obtaining a state license to practice dietetics and to use the protected title of Dietitian.

Does Wyoming have reciprocity for dietitians?

Wyoming has enacted the Dietitian Compact, but compact privileges are not yet issuing. For now, dietitians still apply by endorsement through the state board.

How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to Wyoming?

Most endorsement applications take 1 to 2 Weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

Can I start working in Wyoming while my endorsement application is processing?

A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.
